text.title: Error Handling in VUI Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed when applying error-handling-in-vui; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent re-asks 'I didn't catch that, can you repeat?' three times; user gives up.
antipattern.root-cause: Single fallback prompt used for all rungs.
antipattern.fix: Author distinct rung 1 / rung 2 / rung 3 prompts; rung 2 adds examples.

antipattern.symptom: Silent user hears 'You can say play music' — feels patronising because they weren't unsure, just away from device.
antipattern.root-cause: Same ladder used for silent + misrecognition.
antipattern.fix: No-input ladder asks presence ('Are you still there?'); misrecognition ladder scaffolds vocabulary.

antipattern.symptom: User cycles through 4+ voice retries; never reaches a human; abandons.
antipattern.root-cause: Rung 3 is yet another voice prompt.
antipattern.fix: Rung 3 must escalate channel; if no escalation infrastructure, redesign or reduce intent surface.
